summary: Iran launched missile and drone attacks on US military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, as well as the US Fifth Fleet headquarters, in retaliation for a US strike on an Iranian military position on Qeshm Island and a prior US attack on an Iranian oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM confirmed intercepting several ballistic missiles and drones targeting Bahrain and Kuwait, while also stating that US forces conducted self-defense strikes on Qeshm Island. The conflict escalation has disrupted aviation across Bahrain, Kuwait, and the UAE, with oil prices surpassing $97 per barrel.
key_points: Iran's IRGC launched missile and drone attacks on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters in retaliation for US strikes on Qeshm Island and an Iranian oil tanker. (Source: @VahidOnline/75887)
CENTCOM confirmed US and Bahraini forces intercepted three missiles targeting Bahrain; two missiles fired toward Kuwait failed or disintegrated en route. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92460)
The US conducted a self-defense strike on an Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island following Iranian threats against US forces across the region. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92460)
Flights suspended at Bahrain International Airport, Kuwait International Airport, and airports in Dubai and Abu Dhabi until further notice. (Source: @SharghDaily/163541)
Oil prices surpassed $97 per barrel following Iranian attacks on US bases in Gulf states. (Source: @naya_foriraq/76838)
US Secretary Rubio stated Iran has agreed to discuss nuclear program issues it previously refused to negotiate, and a deal could come soon. (Source: @asafroz/49777)
IDF's Givati Brigade crossed the Litani River and advanced toward Mount Sujud and the Saluki River area as part of a broad operation in southern Lebanon. (Source: @New_security8200/200883)
IDF struck and killed five terror operatives in the Gaza Strip, including Hamas and PIJ commanders involved in planning attacks on Israeli troops. (Source: @manniefabian/58374)

sections.executive_summary: Iran launched a significant missile and drone attack on US military installations in Kuwait, Bahrain, and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters, directly responding to US strikes on Qeshm Island and an Iranian oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. This exchange marks the most direct US-Iran military confrontation in the current conflict cycle, with CENTCOM confirming successful interceptions of multiple ballistic missiles and drones. The escalation has disrupted regional aviation and pushed oil prices above $97 per barrel.

points: The IRGC Aerospace Force launched a combined missile and drone attack targeting the US Fifth Fleet naval headquarters in Bahrain and US forces in Kuwait, in retaliation for US strikes on Qeshm Island and a US attack on an Iranian oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. The IRGC warned that any further aggression would receive a heavier response. (Source: @VahidOnline/75887)
Iranian Revolutionary Guards launched a massive missile and drone attack on US bases in Kuwait in response to the US attack near Qeshm Island. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92453)

The US struck an Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island in a self-defense operation. CENTCOM also shot down three one-way attack drones Iran launched toward civilian vessels in regional waters. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92460)

US Secretary of State Rubio stated Iran has agreed to negotiate on nuclear program issues it previously refused to discuss, and that a deal could happen soon. He also noted Iran's military capability has been severely weakened. (Source: @VahidOnline/75876)
The US reportedly fired on and disabled an Iranian oil tanker attempting to break the US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @N12chat/210642)
Reports from Isfahan indicated air defense activity, likely in response to aerial threats. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72475)

IDF Givati Brigade commander reconstructed the unit's advance to the summit of Mount Sujud (Al-Khiyam area, between Jbeil), noting the brigade crossed the Litani River and advanced toward Mount Sujud and the Saluki River area as part of a broad operation. (Source: @New_security8200/200883)

IDF Arabic-language spokesperson Avichay Adraee warned the Christian population in Tyre that Hezbollah operatives are hiding in the Christian quarter, urging residents to remove them, and stating the IDF would clear the quarter if they remain. (Source: @New_security8200/200885)

IDF Division 91 and the Air Force struck Hezbollah weapons storage facilities, command posts, and additional terror infrastructure in southern Lebanon from which Hezbollah operatives had been operating. (Source: @almogboker78/17278)
Two IDF soldiers were lightly wounded by a Hezbollah explosive drone strike in southern Lebanon. (Source: @inon_yttach/8520)

IDF Home Front Command eased northern border guidelines, allowing schools and workplaces to open in communities along the Lebanon border, with relaxed gathering limits, following the porous ceasefire with Hezbollah. (Source: @manniefabian/58381)
A senior IDF officer at brigadier general rank was removed from his sensitive position following serious moral conduct allegations. (Source: @yediotnews25/130382)

The IDF struck and killed five terror operatives in the Gaza Strip who posed an immediate threat to troops, including a Hamas Shejaiya Battalion member who built explosives and conducted sniper attacks, a Nukhba Force team commander, a Palestinian Islamic Jihad sniper platoon commander, and two Hamas commanders who planted bombs targeting Israeli forces. (Source: @manniefabian/58374)

Al-Qassam Brigades stated that Israeli assassinations of Hamas leaders fail to break the resistance, calling Israel deluded in thinking it can weaken Hamas through targeted killings. (Source: @presstv/192424)
Muslim nations condemned Israeli raids on al-Aqsa Mosque compound, as UN experts warned of escalating settler terror across the occupied territories. (Source: @presstv/192431)
The first day of the fourth round of US-supervised negotiations between Lebanese and Israeli delegations has concluded. (Source: @SharghDaily/163530)
IDF Givati Brigade advanced across the Litani River toward Mount Sujud and the Saluki River area as part of a broad operational push in southern Lebanon. (Source: @New_security8200/200883)

IDF Arabic spokesperson warned Hezbollah operatives are hiding in the Christian quarter of Tyre and urged residents to remove them, threatening the IDF would clear the quarter. (Source: @New_security8200/200885)
IDF Division 91 and the Air Force struck Hezbollah weapons storage facilities, command posts, and terror infrastructure in southern Lebanon. (Source: @almogboker78/17278)
Two IDF soldiers were lightly wounded by a Hezbollah explosive drone in southern Lebanon. (Source: @inon_yttach/8520)
Reports of aerial bombardment over Al-Bazouriyah in southern Lebanon. (Source: @redlinkleb/20506)
Reports of aerial bombardment over Siddiqin in southern Lebanon. (Source: @redlinkleb/20488)
Reports of aerial bombardment over Arab Salim in southern Lebanon. (Source: @redlinkleb/20524)
Iranian attacks confirmed on Erbil in Iraq, as well as Kuwait and Bahrain, but no confirmed attacks on Saudi Arabia or the UAE. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365290)

Iran launched missile and drone attacks on US military bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, with CENTCOM confirming US and Bahraini forces intercepted three missiles targeting Bahrain and two missiles fired toward Kuwait failed or disintegrated. (Source: @SharghDaily/163537)
Three residents in Bahrain reported hearing explosions. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3285)
Kuwaiti air defense systems were reported intercepting missiles and drones. (Source: @N12chat/210644)
Flights suspended at Bahrain International Airport, Kuwait International Airport, and airports in Dubai and Abu Dhabi until further notice. (Source: @SharghDaily/163541)
Oil prices surpassed $97 per barrel following Iranian attacks on US bases in Gulf states. (Source: @naya_foriraq/76838)
US CENTCOM confirmed the US struck an Iranian military position on Qeshm Island and intercepted multiple Iranian missiles and drones targeting Gulf states on June 2. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365295)

key_developments: Iran's IRGC launched a combined missile and drone attack on US military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and the US Fifth Fleet headquarters, in direct retaliation for US strikes on Qeshm Island and a US attack on an Iranian oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: @VahidOnline/75887)

US CENTCOM confirmed that US and Bahraini air defenses intercepted three Iranian ballistic missiles targeting Bahrain, while two missiles fired toward Kuwait failed or disintegrated, and three Iranian one-way attack drones targeting civilian vessels were also shot down. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92460)

The US conducted a self-defense strike on an Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island, with CENTCOM stating no American personnel were harmed in the exchange. (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3286)

Flights were suspended at Bahrain International Airport, Kuwait International Airport, and airports in Dubai and Abu Dhabi until further notice, reflecting the severity of the regional escalation. (Source: @SharghDaily/163541)
Oil prices surpassed $97 per barrel as markets reacted to the Iranian attacks on US bases in Gulf states and the broader escalation in the Strait of Hormuz region. (Source: @naya_foriraq/76838)

IDF Givati Brigade crossed the Litani River and advanced toward Mount Sujud and the Saluki River area in southern Lebanon as part of a broad operational push, while Division 91 and the Air Force struck Hezbollah infrastructure. (Source: @New_security8200/200883)

US Secretary of State Rubio stated Iran has agreed to negotiate on nuclear program issues it previously refused to discuss, suggesting a deal could be imminent, while also monitoring Iran's World Cup delegation for IRGC-linked individuals. (Source: @asafroz/49777)
sections.threat_assessment: The direct exchange of fire between Iran and the US represents the most significant escalation in the current conflict cycle, moving beyond proxy engagements to state-on-state military confrontation. Iran's willingness to target US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain with ballistic missiles and drones signals a substantial shift in its deterrence posture, while the US strike on Qeshm Island demonstrates Washington's readiness to conduct offensive operations on Iranian soil. The disruption of Gulf aviation and the surge in oil prices above $97 per barrel indicate severe regional economic consequences. The IDF's continued operations in southern Lebanon and Gaza, combined with the ongoing US-supervised Lebanon-Israel negotiations, suggest a multi-front pressure campaign against Iranian-backed actors.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states including Bahrain and Kuwait experienced direct missile threats, with their air defense systems actively engaged alongside US forces. The UAE and Saudi Arabia have not reported confirmed attacks, though aviation across the region has been suspended. Kuwait confirmed its air defense systems were activated against incoming missiles and drones. The broader Muslim world has condemned Israeli actions at al-Aqsa, adding diplomatic pressure. The US has maintained its naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, disabling an Iranian oil tanker attempting to break through.
sections.intelligence_notes: The IRGC's claim that the US struck an Iranian oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz prior to the missile attacks introduces a potential trigger event that has not been independently confirmed by US sources. The reported halt in message exchanges between Iran and the US regarding a preliminary understanding, as cited by Fars News, contrasts with Rubio's optimistic statements about a potential nuclear deal, suggesting possible internal divisions or negotiating tactics. The IDF's advance to Mount Sujud and the Saluki River area marks a significant operational milestone in southern Lebanon, potentially threatening Hezbollah's rear positions. The removal of a senior IDF brigadier general on moral grounds during active operations raises questions about command integrity at a critical moment.
